test_cases.description: Verify that a workspace record stores all required fields (slug, git_url, branch, owner_id, org_id, status, created_at, updated_at) in the workspaces table.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
A valid authenticated user exists in the system
test_cases.expected: Row in workspaces table contains exactly: slug='my-workspace', git_url='https://github.com/org/repo', branch='main', owner_id='user-uuid-1234', org_id=NULL, status='active', created_at (non-empty RFC3339), updated_at (non-empty RFC3339)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: row = db.QueryRow('SELECT slug, git_url, branch, owner_id, org_id, status, created_at, updated_at FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', 'my-workspace'); assert row.slug == 'my-workspace'; assert row.git_url == 'https://github.com/org/repo'; assert row.branch == 'main'; assert row.owner_id == 'user-uuid-1234'; assert row.org_id == null; assert row.status == 'active'; assert isValidRFC3339(row.created_at); assert isValidRFC3339(row.updated_at)

test_cases.description: Verify that the slug validator accepts slugs that are 3–64 chars, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, start with a letter, have no trailing hyphen, and have no consecutive hyphens.
preconditions: Slug validator function is available
test_cases.expected: All provided slugs pass validation without error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each slug in valid_slugs { result = validateSlug(slug); assert result.isValid == true; assert result.error == nil }

test_cases.description: Verify that the slug validator rejects slugs failing format, length, leading-character, trailing-hyphen, or consecutive-hyphen rules with HTTP 400.
preconditions: Slug validator is integrated with POST /api/v1/workspaces handler
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 response with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}} for each invalid slug
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each slug in invalid_slugs { resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: slug, git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.body.error.code == 400; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string }

test_cases.description: Verify that the git_url validator accepts valid HTTPS and SSH URLs with non-empty host and path.
preconditions: git_url validator function is available
test_cases.expected: All provided git_url values pass validation without error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each url in valid_urls { result = validateGitURL(url); assert result.isValid == true; assert result.error == nil }

test_cases.description: Verify that the git_url validator rejects empty URLs, plain HTTP URLs, URLs with empty host, and URLs with empty path with HTTP 400.
preconditions: git_url validator is integrated with POST /api/v1/workspaces handler
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 response with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}} for each invalid git_url
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each url in invalid_urls { resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'valid-slug', git_url: url}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.body.error.code == 400; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string }

test_cases.description: Verify that the branch validator accepts valid git ref names that comply with all git ref naming rules.
preconditions: branch validator function is available
test_cases.expected: All provided branch values pass validation without error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each branch in valid_branches { result = validateBranch(branch); assert result.isValid == true; assert result.error == nil }

test_cases.description: Verify that the branch validator rejects branch values that violate git ref naming rules with HTTP 400.
preconditions: branch validator is integrated with POST /api/v1/workspaces handler
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 response with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}} for each invalid branch
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each branch in invalid_branches { resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'valid-slug', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo', branch: branch}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.body.error.code == 400; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string }

test_cases.description: Verify that the same git_url can appear in multiple workspaces with different slugs.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: Both workspaces are created successfully with HTTP 201; the workspaces table contains two rows with the same git_url but different slugs
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp1 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', workspace_a, userApiKey); assert resp1.statusCode == 201; resp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', workspace_b, userApiKey); assert resp2.statusCode == 201; rows = db.Query('SELECT slug FROM workspaces WHERE git_url = ?', 'https://github.com/org/repo'); assert len(rows) == 2; assert rows contains 'ws-alice' and 'ws-bob'

test_cases.description: Verify that the workspaces table stores status as 'active' or 'archived' only, and never writes 'deleted'.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
An archived workspace exists with slug 'ws-to-delete'
test_cases.expected: After DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/ws-to-delete, no row with slug 'ws-to-delete' exists in the workspaces table; no row with status='deleted' is present in the table at any time
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/ws-to-delete', ownerApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 204; row = db.QueryRow('SELECT status FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', 'ws-to-delete'); assert row == nil; deletedRows = db.Query('SELECT * FROM workspaces WHERE status = ?', 'deleted'); assert len(deletedRows) == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that workspace records store created_at and updated_at as valid RFC 3339 strings in the workspaces table.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: Row in workspaces table has created_at and updated_at as non-empty strings parseable as RFC 3339 timestamps
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'ts-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, userApiKey); row = db.QueryRow('SELECT created_at, updated_at FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', 'ts-ws'); assert parseRFC3339(row.created_at) != nil; assert parseRFC3339(row.updated_at) != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that on server boot, the workspaces table is created with the correct schema using CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS via the apikit DB abstraction.
preconditions: SQLite database file exists but the workspaces table does not
apikit.OpenDatabase returns a valid *apikit.DB
test_cases.expected: After server boot sequence completes, the workspaces table exists with columns: slug (TEXT PRIMARY KEY), git_url (TEXT NOT NULL), branch (TEXT), owner_id (TEXT NOT NULL), org_id (TEXT), status (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'), created_at (TEXT NOT NULL), updated_at (TEXT NOT NULL)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openTestDatabase(); initializeSchema(db); rows = db.Query("PRAGMA table_info(workspaces)"); assert len(rows) == 8; assert columnExists(rows, 'slug', 'TEXT', isPrimaryKey=true); assert columnExists(rows, 'git_url', 'TEXT', notNull=true); assert columnExists(rows, 'branch', 'TEXT', notNull=false); assert columnExists(rows, 'owner_id', 'TEXT', notNull=true); assert columnExists(rows, 'org_id', 'TEXT', notNull=false); assert columnExists(rows, 'status', 'TEXT', notNull=true, default='active'); assert columnExists(rows, 'created_at', 'TEXT', notNull=true); assert columnExists(rows, 'updated_at', 'TEXT', notNull=true)

test_cases.description: Verify that when the workspaces table already exists at boot time, the server proceeds without error due to IF NOT EXISTS semantics.
preconditions: SQLite database file has the workspaces table already created
Server boot sequence is invoked again
test_cases.expected: Boot sequence returns nil error; server starts normally without any schema conflict error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openTestDatabase(); initializeSchema(db); err = initializeSchema(db); assert err == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that MountHandlers registers the workspaces:read permission scope (Permission{Resource: 'workspaces', Action: 'read'}) with apikit's permission registry.
preconditions: apikit server instance is created
SQLite database is initialised
test_cases.expected: After MountHandlers is called, the permission 'workspaces:read' (Resource='workspaces', Action='read') is registered in apikit's PAT permission registry
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = newTestServer(db); err = server.MountHandlers(db, workspacesReadPermission, workspacesCreatePermission); assert err == nil; registeredPerms = apikit.GetRegisteredPermissions(); assert registeredPerms contains Permission{Resource: 'workspaces', Action: 'read'}

test_cases.description: Verify that MountHandlers registers the workspaces:create permission scope (Permission{Resource: 'workspaces', Action: 'create'}) with apikit's permission registry.
preconditions: apikit server instance is created
SQLite database is initialised
test_cases.expected: After MountHandlers is called, the permission 'workspaces:create' (Resource='workspaces', Action='create') is registered in apikit's PAT permission registry
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = newTestServer(db); err = server.MountHandlers(db, workspacesReadPermission, workspacesCreatePermission); assert err == nil; registeredPerms = apikit.GetRegisteredPermissions(); assert registeredPerms contains Permission{Resource: 'workspaces', Action: 'create'}

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT with workspaces:read scope grants read-only access to list and view the PAT owner's workspaces.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' exists with API key and two workspaces
PAT with workspaces:read scope issued for user 'alice'
test_cases.expected: GET /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 200 with JSON array containing only alice's workspaces; GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug returns HTTP 200 for alice's workspace
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', patReadToken); assert resp.statusCode == 200; assert all(w.owner_id == alice.id for w in resp.body); resp2 = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws', patReadToken); assert resp2.statusCode == 200

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT with workspaces:create scope can create workspaces and read the owner's own workspaces.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' exists with API key
PAT with workspaces:create scope issued for user 'alice'
test_cases.expected: POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 201; GET /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 200 with alice's workspaces
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'new-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, patCreateToken); assert resp.statusCode == 201; resp2 = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', patCreateToken); assert resp2.statusCode == 200; assert any(w.slug == 'new-ws' for w in resp2.body)

test_cases.description: Verify that admin tokens have full read and write access to all workspaces (except creation).
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns workspace 'alice-ws' with status active
Admin token is available
test_cases.expected: GET /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 200 with all workspaces; GET /api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws returns HTTP 200; POST /api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/archive returns HTTP 200
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', adminToken); assert resp.statusCode == 200; resp2 = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws', adminToken); assert resp2.statusCode == 200; resp3 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/archive', {}, adminToken); assert resp3.statusCode == 200

test_cases.description: Verify that using an admin token to create a workspace returns HTTP 403 because workspaces require a real user as owner.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
Admin token is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'admin-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, adminToken); assert resp.statusCode == 403; assert resp.body.error.code == 403; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

test_cases.description: Verify that a user API key holder has full access to their own workspaces and can create workspaces.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' exists with a valid API key
test_cases.expected: POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 201; all lifecycle operations on own workspace return appropriate success codes
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'alice-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 201; resp2 = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws', aliceApiKey); assert resp2.statusCode == 200; resp3 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/archive', {}, aliceApiKey); assert resp3.statusCode == 200

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT with workspaces:read grants read-only access to the PAT owner's workspaces only.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns workspace 'alice-ws'
User 'bob' owns workspace 'bob-ws'
PAT with workspaces:read issued for 'alice'
test_cases.expected: GET /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 200 with only alice's workspaces; GET /api/v1/workspaces/bob-ws returns HTTP 404
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', aliceReadPAT); assert resp.statusCode == 200; assert all(w.owner_id == alice.id for w in resp.body); resp2 = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/bob-ws', aliceReadPAT); assert resp2.statusCode == 404

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT with workspaces:create can create workspaces on behalf of the owner and read the owner's own workspaces.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' exists with API key
PAT with workspaces:create issued for 'alice'
test_cases.expected: POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 201 with owner_id set to alice's user ID; GET /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 200 with alice's workspaces
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'created-via-pat', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, aliceCreatePAT); assert resp.statusCode == 201; assert resp.body.owner_id == alice.id; resp2 = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', aliceCreatePAT); assert resp2.statusCode == 200

test_cases.description: Verify that when a workspace exists but the requesting credential cannot access it, the API returns 404 (not 403) to prevent slug enumeration.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'bob' owns workspace 'bob-secret-ws'
User 'alice' exists with a valid API key and no access to 'bob-secret-ws'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error":{"code":404,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/bob-secret-ws', aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 404; assert resp.body.error.code == 404; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

test_cases.description: Verify that requests with missing, invalid, or expired credentials are rejected with HTTP 401.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
test_cases.expected: HTTP 401 with JSON body {"error":{"code":401,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', noCredential); assert resp.statusCode == 401; assert resp.body.error.code == 401; resp2 = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', invalidToken); assert resp2.statusCode == 401; assert resp2.body.error.code == 401

test_cases.description: Verify that a valid credential lacking the required permission scope for an endpoint is rejected with HTTP 403.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns workspace 'alice-ws'
PAT with workspaces:read issued for 'alice' (no archive/delete scope)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/archive', {}, aliceReadPAT); assert resp.statusCode == 403; assert resp.body.error.code == 403; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/workspaces creates a workspace with status active, sets owner_id to the creating user, and returns HTTP 201 with the workspace JSON object.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' exists with a valid API key
SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with JSON body containing slug='new-workspace', git_url='https://github.com/org/repo', status='active', owner_id=alice.id, created_at (RFC3339), updated_at (RFC3339)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'new-workspace', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 201; assert resp.body.slug == 'new-workspace'; assert resp.body.git_url == 'https://github.com/org/repo'; assert resp.body.status == 'active'; assert resp.body.owner_id == alice.id; assert parseRFC3339(resp.body.created_at) != nil; assert parseRFC3339(resp.body.updated_at) != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that creating a workspace with a duplicate slug returns HTTP 409.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
Workspace with slug 'existing-ws' already exists
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 409 with JSON body {"error":{"code":409,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'existing-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo2'}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 409; assert resp.body.error.code == 409; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

test_cases.description: Verify that providing an org_id when the creating user is not a member of that organization returns HTTP 403.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' exists with API key but is NOT a member of organization 'other-org'
Organization 'other-org' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'org-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo', org_id: 'other-org-uuid'}, aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 403; assert resp.body.error.code == 403; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

test_cases.description: Verify that providing an org_id that references a non-existent organization returns HTTP 400.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'org-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo', org_id: 'nonexistent-org-uuid'}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.body.error.code == 400; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

test_cases.description: Verify that a missing or malformed request body returns HTTP 400.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', malformedBody, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.body.error.code == 400; resp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', null, userApiKey); assert resp2.statusCode == 400; assert resp2.body.error.code == 400

test_cases.description: Verify that when a valid branch is provided in the create request, it is stored and returned in the workspace object.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with JSON body where branch='feature/my-feature'; workspaces table row has branch='feature/my-feature'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'branched-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo', branch: 'feature/my-feature'}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 201; assert resp.body.branch == 'feature/my-feature'; row = db.QueryRow('SELECT branch FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', 'branched-ws'); assert row.branch == 'feature/my-feature'

test_cases.description: Verify that when branch is omitted from the create request, the workspace stores NULL for branch and returns branch: null.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with JSON body where branch is null; workspaces table row has branch=NULL
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'no-branch-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 201; assert resp.body.branch == null; row = db.QueryRow('SELECT branch FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', 'no-branch-ws'); assert row.branch == null

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/workspaces with admin token returns all workspaces across all users, excluding archived by default, ordered by created_at descending.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' (active), 'alice-archived' (archived)
User 'bob' owns 'bob-ws' (active)
Admin token is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON array containing 'alice-ws' and 'bob-ws' but NOT 'alice-archived'; array ordered by created_at descending
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', adminToken); assert resp.statusCode == 200; slugs = [w.slug for w in resp.body]; assert 'alice-ws' in slugs; assert 'bob-ws' in slugs; assert 'alice-archived' not in slugs; assert isSortedByCreatedAtDesc(resp.body)

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/workspaces with user API key or PAT returns only the authenticated user's workspaces, excluding archived by default.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' (active) and 'alice-archived' (archived)
User 'bob' owns 'bob-ws'
Alice's user API key and PAT with workspaces:read are available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON array containing only 'alice-ws' (not 'alice-archived' or 'bob-ws'), ordered by created_at descending
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; slugs = [w.slug for w in resp.body]; assert 'alice-ws' in slugs; assert 'alice-archived' not in slugs; assert 'bob-ws' not in slugs; assert isSortedByCreatedAtDesc(resp.body)

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/workspaces?include_archived=true includes archived workspaces in the result set.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' (active) and 'alice-archived' (archived)
Alice's user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON array containing both 'alice-ws' and 'alice-archived'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces?include_archived=true', aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; slugs = [w.slug for w in resp.body]; assert 'alice-ws' in slugs; assert 'alice-archived' in slugs

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/workspaces returns an empty array when no workspaces match the query criteria.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' exists with API key but owns no workspaces
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON body []
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; assert resp.body == []

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug returns the workspace record for an authorized credential.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws'
Alice's user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON workspace object where slug='alice-ws'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws', aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; assert resp.body.slug == 'alice-ws'; assert resp.body.owner_id == alice.id

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug returns HTTP 404 when no workspace exists with the given slug.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
No workspace with slug 'nonexistent-ws' exists
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error":{"code":404,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/nonexistent-ws', userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 404; assert resp.body.error.code == 404; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug returns HTTP 404 (not 403) when the workspace exists but the credential doesn't own it.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'bob' owns 'bob-ws'
User 'alice' exists with API key and does NOT own 'bob-ws'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error":{"code":404,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/bob-ws', aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 404; assert resp.body.error.code == 404; assert resp.statusCode != 403

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/archive updates the workspace status to 'archived' and returns HTTP 200 with the updated object.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' with status 'active'
Alice's user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON workspace object where status='archived'; workspaces table row updated_at is updated
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/archive', {}, aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; assert resp.body.status == 'archived'; assert resp.body.slug == 'alice-ws'; row = db.QueryRow('SELECT status FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', 'alice-ws'); assert row.status == 'archived'

test_cases.description: Verify that archiving an already-archived workspace returns HTTP 400, not a no-op.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' with status 'archived'
Alice's user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/archive', {}, aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.body.error.code == 400; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT (workspaces:read or workspaces:create) attempting to archive a workspace is rejected with HTTP 403.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' with status 'active'
PAT with workspaces:read issued for 'alice'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/archive', {}, aliceReadPAT); assert resp.statusCode == 403; assert resp.body.error.code == 403; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

test_cases.description: Verify that archiving a non-existent workspace or one the credential cannot access returns HTTP 404.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
No workspace with slug 'ghost-ws' exists
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error":{"code":404,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/ghost-ws/archive', {}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 404; assert resp.body.error.code == 404; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/reactivate updates an archived workspace to 'active' and returns HTTP 200 with the updated object.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' with status 'archived'
Alice's user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON workspace object where status='active'; workspaces table row shows status='active'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/reactivate', {}, aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; assert resp.body.status == 'active'; assert resp.body.slug == 'alice-ws'; row = db.QueryRow('SELECT status FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', 'alice-ws'); assert row.status == 'active'

test_cases.description: Verify that reactivating an already-active workspace returns HTTP 400, not a no-op.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' with status 'active'
Alice's user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT attempting to reactivate a workspace is rejected with HTTP 403.

PAT with workspaces:create issued for 'alice'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}

test_cases.description: Verify that reactivating a non-existent workspace or one the credential cannot access returns HTTP 404.

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug physically removes the row from the workspaces table and returns HTTP 204 with empty body.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' with status 'archived'

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws', aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 204; assert resp.body == ''; row = db.QueryRow('SELECT * FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', 'alice-ws'); assert row == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to delete an active workspace returns HTTP 400.

Alice's user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}

test_cases.description: Verify that a PAT attempting to delete a workspace is rejected with HTTP 403.

test_cases.description: Verify that workspace deletion performs a physical delete removing the row from the workspaces table with no soft-delete flag or audit record retained.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
An archived workspace row exists with slug 'to-delete'
test_cases.expected: After DELETE, the workspaces table contains no row with slug 'to-delete' and no row with status='deleted'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: deleteWorkspace(db, 'to-delete'); rows = db.Query('SELECT * FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', 'to-delete'); assert len(rows) == 0; deletedRows = db.Query('SELECT * FROM workspaces WHERE status = ?', 'deleted'); assert len(deletedRows) == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that 'afc workspace create --git-url <url> --slug <slug>' calls POST /api/v1/workspaces and prints the workspace JSON to stdout, exiting 0.
preconditions: afc binary is built and available
Server is running
User is authenticated with a valid API key
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; stdout contains valid JSON workspace object with slug='my-ws'; stderr is empty or contains no error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result = runCLI(['workspace', 'create', '--git-url', 'https://github.com/org/repo', '--slug', 'my-ws']); assert result.exitCode == 0; ws = parseJSON(result.stdout); assert ws.slug == 'my-ws'; assert ws.status == 'active'

test_cases.description: Verify that 'afc workspace create --org <org-slug>' resolves the org slug to a UUID before calling the API and the workspace includes org_id.
preconditions: afc binary is built and available
Server is running
User is authenticated with a valid API key
Organization 'my-org' exists with UUID 'my-org-uuid'
User is a member of 'my-org'

input.command: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug org-ws --org my-org

test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; stdout contains valid JSON workspace object with org_id='my-org-uuid'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result = runCLI(['workspace', 'create', '--git-url', 'https://github.com/org/repo', '--slug', 'org-ws', '--org', 'my-org']); assert result.exitCode == 0; ws = parseJSON(result.stdout); assert ws.org_id == 'my-org-uuid'

test_cases.description: Verify that 'afc workspace create' with missing --git-url or --slug prints a usage hint to stderr and exits 1 without making an API call.
preconditions: afc binary is built and available
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains usage hint; no HTTP request made to POST /api/v1/workspaces
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result = runCLI(['workspace', 'create', '--slug', 'my-ws']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains usage hint; assert noAPICallWasMade(POST, '/api/v1/workspaces')

test_cases.description: Verify that 'afc workspace create --org <nonexistent-slug>' prints an error to stderr and exits 1 without making the workspace create API call.
preconditions: afc binary is built and available
Server is running
User is authenticated

input.command: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug my-ws --org nonexistent-org

test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains error about org resolution failure; no POST /api/v1/workspaces call made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result = runCLI(['workspace', 'create', '--git-url', 'https://github.com/org/repo', '--slug', 'my-ws', '--org', 'nonexistent-org']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains error message; assert noAPICallWasMade(POST, '/api/v1/workspaces')

test_cases.description: Verify that the afc CLI builds its command tree from RootCommand() and includes LoginCmd, UserCmd, KeysCmd, TokensCmd, OrgsCmd, AdminCmd, and the workspace subcommand group.
preconditions: afc binary is built and available
test_cases.expected: Help output lists 'workspace' as a subcommand and includes all standard apikit commands (login, user, keys, tokens, orgs, admin)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result = runCLI(['--help']); assert result.exitCode == 0; assert result.stdout contains 'workspace'; assert result.stdout contains 'login'; assert result.stdout contains 'user'; assert result.stdout contains 'keys'; assert result.stdout contains 'tokens'; assert result.stdout contains 'orgs'; assert result.stdout contains 'admin'

test_cases.description: Verify that the afc CLI uses CLIExecute() for execution, CLIPrintError() for errors, and CLIExitCode() to derive the process exit code, returning 0 on success and 1 on error.
preconditions: afc binary source code is available for inspection
afc binary is built
test_cases.expected: Successful command exits with code 0; failed command exits with code 1; error output goes to stderr via CLIPrintError
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: successResult = runCLI(['workspace', 'list']); assert successResult.exitCode == 0; errorResult = runCLI(['workspace', 'get', 'nonexistent-slug']); assert errorResult.exitCode == 1; assert errorResult.stderr is non-empty

test_cases.description: Verify that afc CLI library functions signal errors via return values using Go's (*T, error) idiom and never call os.Exit directly.
preconditions: afc source code is available for static analysis
test_cases.expected: No call to os.Exit appears in any library function (only in main()); all library functions return (*T, error) tuples for errors
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: sourceFiles = getAllGoSourceFiles('internal/', 'pkg/'); for each file in sourceFiles { assert not containsPattern(file, 'os.Exit') }; mainFile = getGoSourceFile('cmd/afc/main.go'); assert containsPattern(mainFile, 'os.Exit') == allowed

test_cases.description: Verify that all error responses use apikit's standard JSON envelope format {"error":{"code":<HTTP_STATUS>,"message":"Human-readable description"}}.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
Authenticated user API key is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 response body is valid JSON with keys 'error.code' (integer matching HTTP status) and 'error.message' (non-empty string)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'INVALID SLUG', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; body = parseJSON(resp.body); assert body.error != nil; assert body.error.code == 400; assert isInt(body.error.code); assert isNonEmptyString(body.error.message)

property_tests.description: For any set of workspace create requests with the same slug, only one can succeed; the slug is unique across all rows at any point in time.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate N (2–10) concurrent or sequential POST /api/v1/workspaces requests all using the same slug but different git_urls from distinct authenticated users
property_tests.invariant_check: Exactly one request returns HTTP 201; all others return HTTP 409; at any checkpoint during or after execution, db.Query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', slug) returns exactly 1

property_tests.description: For any workspace record in the workspaces table, the owner_id is always non-null and references a real user identity, never an admin-token identity.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary sequences of workspace create requests using user API keys and attempt creates with admin tokens; inspect all resulting rows
property_tests.invariant_check: For every row in the workspaces table: row.owner_id != nil AND row.owner_id is a valid user UUID AND row.owner_id does not match any known admin token identity; any admin-token create attempt results in HTTP 403 and no new row

property_tests.description: For any workspace record in the workspaces table, the status column contains exactly 'active' or 'archived'; the value 'deleted' is never present.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary sequences of create, archive, reactivate, and delete operations on workspaces; after each operation, inspect all remaining rows
property_tests.invariant_check: For every row in the workspaces table at any point: row.status in {'active', 'archived'}; db.Query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces WHERE status NOT IN ("active", "archived")') == 0

property_tests.description: For any DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug request, a workspace row is physically removed only when its status was 'archived' at the time of deletion; active workspace delete is always rejected.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary workspaces in active and archived states; for each, attempt DELETE and inspect the database before and after
property_tests.invariant_check: If workspace.status == 'archived' before DELETE: request returns HTTP 204, row is absent after; if workspace.status == 'active' before DELETE: request returns HTTP 400, row is still present after

property_tests.description: For any GET /api/v1/workspaces response for a non-admin credential, all returned workspaces have owner_id equal to the authenticated user's ID.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Create workspaces for multiple users (user-A, user-B, user-C); for each user, call GET /api/v1/workspaces with their API key or PAT
property_tests.invariant_check: For all workspace objects W in response for user U: W.owner_id == U.id; no W in response where W.owner_id != U.id

property_tests.description: For any GET /api/v1/workspaces response when include_archived is absent or false, no archived workspace appears in the response array.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Create arbitrary mixes of active and archived workspaces for a user; call GET /api/v1/workspaces without ?include_archived or with ?include_archived=false
property_tests.invariant_check: For all workspace objects W in response: W.status == 'active'; len([W for W in response if W.status == 'archived']) == 0

property_tests.description: For any workspace record in the workspaces table, created_at and updated_at are always valid RFC 3339 timestamp strings and are never null or empty.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary workspace create, archive, and reactivate operations; after each operation, query all affected rows
property_tests.invariant_check: For every row in workspaces table: row.created_at != nil AND row.created_at != '' AND parseRFC3339(row.created_at) != error; row.updated_at != nil AND row.updated_at != '' AND parseRFC3339(row.updated_at) != error

property_tests.description: For any request for a workspace the credential cannot access, the API always returns HTTP 404 regardless of whether the workspace exists, never HTTP 403.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate pairs of (credential, slug) where the credential either has no access or the workspace doesn't exist; for each pair, issue GET, archive, reactivate, and delete requests
property_tests.invariant_check: For all such requests: response.statusCode == 404; response.statusCode != 403; response.body.error.code == 404

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the slug validator rejects slugs of exactly 2 characters (below minimum) and exactly 65 characters (above maximum) with HTTP 400.
preconditions: Slug validator is integrated with POST /api/v1/workspaces handler
Authenticated user API key is available
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}} for both the 2-char and 65-char slugs
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp1 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'ab', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, userApiKey); assert resp1.statusCode == 400; assert resp1.body.error.code == 400; resp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz012', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, userApiKey); assert resp2.statusCode == 400; assert resp2.body.error.code == 400

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the slug validator rejects slugs starting with a digit or hyphen with HTTP 400.
preconditions: Slug validator is integrated with POST /api/v1/workspaces handler
Authenticated user API key is available
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}} for both digit-start and hyphen-start slugs
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp1 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: '1workspace', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, userApiKey); assert resp1.statusCode == 400; assert resp1.body.error.code == 400; resp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: '-workspace', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, userApiKey); assert resp2.statusCode == 400; assert resp2.body.error.code == 400

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the branch validator rejects an explicitly provided empty string branch with HTTP 400.
preconditions: branch validator is integrated with POST /api/v1/workspaces handler
Authenticated user API key is available
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'my-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo', branch: ''}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.body.error.code == 400; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the git_url validator rejects plain HTTP URLs (http://) with HTTP 400.
preconditions: git_url validator is integrated with POST /api/v1/workspaces handler
Authenticated user API key is available
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'my-ws', git_url: 'http://github.com/org/repo'}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 400; assert resp.body.error.code == 400; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that if the database is unavailable or the CREATE TABLE statement fails during boot, the boot sequence returns a non-nil error and the server does not start.
preconditions: SQLite database path is configured to an inaccessible location or database is locked
edge_case_tests.expected: Boot function returns a non-nil error; server does not start and does not serve any requests
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: err = initializeSchema(unavailableDB); assert err != nil; assert serverIsNotListening()

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a PAT with workspaces:read attempting to archive, reactivate, or delete a workspace is rejected with HTTP 403.

User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' with status 'active'
PAT with workspaces:read issued for 'alice'
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}} for each of archive, reactivate, and delete
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp1 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/archive', {}, aliceReadPAT); assert resp1.statusCode == 403; assert resp1.body.error.code == 403; resp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/reactivate', {}, aliceReadPAT); assert resp2.statusCode == 403; assert resp2.body.error.code == 403; resp3 = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws', aliceReadPAT); assert resp3.statusCode == 403; assert resp3.body.error.code == 403

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a PAT with workspaces:create attempting to archive, reactivate, or delete a workspace is rejected with HTTP 403.

User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' with status 'active'
PAT with workspaces:create issued for 'alice'
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}} for each of archive, reactivate, and delete
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp1 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/archive', {}, aliceCreatePAT); assert resp1.statusCode == 403; assert resp1.body.error.code == 403; resp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws/reactivate', {}, aliceCreatePAT); assert resp2.statusCode == 403; assert resp2.body.error.code == 403; resp3 = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws', aliceCreatePAT); assert resp3.statusCode == 403; assert resp3.body.error.code == 403

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that an admin token attempting to call POST /api/v1/workspaces is rejected with HTTP 403.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
Admin token is available
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'admin-created-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, adminToken); assert resp.statusCode == 403; assert resp.body.error.code == 403; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a PAT with workspaces:read (but not workspaces:create) attempting to create a workspace is rejected with HTTP 403.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' exists with API key
PAT with workspaces:read issued for 'alice'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'read-pat-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, aliceReadPAT); assert resp.statusCode == 403; assert resp.body.error.code == 403; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a transient database insert failure after validation passes returns HTTP 500 with no partial workspace record retained.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
Database is configured to fail INSERT operations (mock/stub)
Authenticated user API key is available
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500 with JSON body {"error":{"code":500,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}; no row with slug 'db-fail-ws' in workspaces table
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockDB.failNextInsert(); resp = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', {slug: 'db-fail-ws', git_url: 'https://github.com/org/repo'}, userApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 500; assert resp.body.error.code == 500; row = db.QueryRow('SELECT * FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', 'db-fail-ws'); assert row == nil

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/workspaces returns all matching workspaces without pagination, with no pagination headers or cursors in the response.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 25 active workspaces
Alice's user API key is available
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON array of exactly 25 workspace objects; no Link header, X-Next-Cursor, or pagination field in response
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 200; assert len(resp.body) == 25; assert resp.headers['Link'] == nil; assert resp.headers['X-Next-Cursor'] == nil; assert resp.body is array (not object with 'data'/'cursor')

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a database DELETE statement failure after authorization passes returns HTTP 500.
preconditions: Server is running with workspace handlers mounted
User 'alice' owns 'alice-ws' with status 'archived'
Database is configured to fail DELETE operations (mock/stub)
Alice's user API key is available
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockDB.failNextDelete(); resp = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/alice-ws', aliceApiKey); assert resp.statusCode == 500; assert resp.body.error.code == 500; assert resp.body.error.message is non-empty string

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when org slug resolution returns a success status but with a missing or null org UUID, the CLI treats it as a resolution failure and exits 1 without making the workspace create API call.
preconditions: afc binary is built and available
Org resolution API is mocked to return success with null/missing UUID

input.command: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug my-ws --org bad-org

edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains error message about resolution failure; no POST /api/v1/workspaces call made
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockOrgResolution.returnNullUUID(); result = runCLI(['workspace', 'create', '--git-url', 'https://github.com/org/repo', '--slug', 'my-ws', '--org', 'bad-org']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains error message; assert noAPICallWasMade(POST, '/api/v1/workspaces')

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when org slug resolution returns an unexpected data shape, the CLI exits 1 with an error to stderr and does not make the workspace create API call.
preconditions: afc binary is built and available
Org resolution API is mocked to return malformed response

input.command: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug my-ws --org bad-org

edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains error message about unexpected response; no POST /api/v1/workspaces call made
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockOrgResolution.returnMalformedBody(); result = runCLI(['workspace', 'create', '--git-url', 'https://github.com/org/repo', '--slug', 'my-ws', '--org', 'bad-org']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains error message; assert noAPICallWasMade(POST, '/api/v1/workspaces')

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: create a workspace via the afc CLI using HTTPS git_url and verify it is persisted in the workspaces table with correct fields.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug my-workspace' with a valid user API key
mockable: apikit organization membership check (if org_id not provided)
expected_effects: afc CLI exits 0
stdout contains JSON workspace object with slug='my-workspace', status='active', owner_id matching alice's user ID
workspaces table contains exactly one row with slug='my-workspace' and status='active'
created_at and updated_at are valid RFC 3339 strings

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: archive then permanently delete a workspace via the afc CLI; verify the slug is freed and no row remains in the workspaces table.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'afc workspace archive my-workspace' followed by 'afc workspace delete my-workspace --confirm' with the workspace owner's API key
expected_effects: After archive: afc exits 0, stdout contains JSON with status='archived', workspaces table row has status='archived'
After delete: afc exits 0, stderr contains confirmation message, no row with slug 'my-workspace' exists in workspaces table
Slug 'my-workspace' can be reused: subsequent POST /api/v1/workspaces with same slug returns HTTP 201

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: list workspaces with --include-archived and verify both active and archived workspaces appear in the JSON array output.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'afc workspace list --include-archived' with the workspace owner's API key when the user has both active and archived workspaces
expected_effects: afc exits 0
stdout contains valid JSON array
Array includes workspaces with status='active' and status='archived'
Array contains only workspaces owned by the authenticated user
Array is ordered by created_at descending

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: create a workspace with org association via the afc CLI using --org slug; verify org UUID is resolved and stored in the workspace record.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'afc workspace create --git-url git@github.com:org/repo --slug team-ws --org my-org' with a user API key where the user is a member of 'my-org'
mockable: Org slug-to-UUID resolution API call (may use apikit SDK or direct HTTP)
expected_effects: afc exits 0
stdout contains JSON workspace object with org_id equal to 'my-org' UUID
workspaces table row has org_id set to 'my-org' UUID

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: a PAT with workspaces:read scope reads the PAT owner's workspaces and does not see workspaces belonging to other users.
smoke_tests.trigger: Call GET /api/v1/workspaces with a PAT carrying workspaces:read scope issued for user 'alice'
real_components: apikit auth middleware (PAT validation and scope check)
expected_effects: HTTP 200 returned
Response JSON array contains only workspaces where owner_id matches alice's user ID
No workspace belonging to other users appears in the array
Archived workspaces are excluded by default

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: reactivate an archived workspace via the afc CLI and verify the workspace status is updated to 'active' in the database.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'afc workspace reactivate my-workspace' with the workspace owner's API key when 'my-workspace' has status 'archived'
expected_effects: afc exits 0
stdout contains JSON workspace object with status='active'
workspaces table row for 'my-workspace' has status='active'
updated_at is updated to a time after the reactivate request
